retry_reason: btrfs_try_tree_read_lock is a non-blocking trylock; if it fails, the lock is contended, retrying later may succeed.
contract_summary: This function tries to acquire a read lock on the root node and return it with a reference held. It may retry internally if the root node changes under it. On success, the caller gets an extent_buffer that is read-locked and has an elevated refcount. The caller must eventually call btrfs_tree_read_unlock and free_extent_buffer (or btrfs_tree_read_unlock then free_extent_buffer). On failure (lock not acquired), it returns ERR_PTR(-EAGAIN) without any reference held.

judge.reason: Primary purpose is to acquire a read-locked reference to the root node; all logic is about obtaining and maintaining that reference with appropriate lock and refcount management.

check.reason: The function calls btrfs_root_node which is a get function on the return value (eb). The returned eb is then returned to the caller without dropping the reference, so the caller obtains a reference. The access path eb.refs.refs and type chain extent_buffer--refcount_t--atomic_t match the callee information and the code.
